zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Foljambe於1365年出生在英國德比夏爾哈索普時，她的父親Godfrey de Foljambe II22歲，母親Margaret de Villiers21歲。她與Nicholas Montgomery Lord of Cubley結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1453年3月17日在英國英格蘭越可爾享年88歲。

nameMeanings.name: Foljambe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: nickname from Old French fol ‘foolish, silly’ + jambe ‘leg’, probably a nickname for a lame person, someone with a maimed or crooked leg; compare Belgian . In Old French fol could be used of something useless or of little value, e.g. farine folle ‘mill dust’.

variants.name: Fulljames
meanings: English: variant of Foljambe with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Belgian
meanings: Norman, English, Scottish: nickname from Anglo-Norman French bele jambe ‘fine leg’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Foljambe was born in 1365, in Hassop, Derbyshire, England, her father, Godfrey de Foljambe II, was 22 and her mother, Margaret de Villiers, was 21. She married Nicholas Montgomery Lord of Cubley. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 17 March 1453, in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 88.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Foljambe est née en 1365, à Hassop, Derbyshire, Angleterre, son père, Godfrey de Foljambe II, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Margaret de Villiers, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Nicholas Montgomery Lord of Cubley. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 17 mars 1453, à Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 88 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Foljambe(이)가 1365년 영국 더비실르 하소프에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Godfrey de Foljambe II(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret de Villiers(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 Nicholas Montgomery Lord of Cubley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1453년 3월 17일 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르에서 88세의 나이로 사망했다.

Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Nicholas Montgomery Lord of Cubley. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь.
